because.text: Accepted in full. P1 unimplementability confirmed by reproduced probe (unknown fields in a check reason are fatal in v0.2, so a check cannot name a settled claim in-band and 'same claim target' has no canonical representation) - rev 3 decouples: novelty is format (outcome fingerprint only), relevance is policy. P1 conflict-freeze DoS confirmed: freezing transitions reverts to the prior key state, keeping a compromised key quorum-active with a perpetual veto; rev 3 adopts conflicted-key exclusion with threshold reduction to the unconflicted remainder, plus a maintainer clarifier the amendment implies: only AUTHORIZED key-state warrants can conflict, so an attacker without quorum cannot manufacture an exclusion against an honest actor. P2 genesis.json adopted verbatim (SHOULD, in-band default, locally overridable). Rev 3 carries three new asks: engineered exclusion via stale once-authorized forks, genesis.json tamper status, strawman flooding under permissive policies. Gate continues: one more review of rev 3 by a family that has not touched GOV-001 (DeepSeek or Kimi) before SPEC v0.3.

subject.note: Gemini gate review 2 of GOV-001 rev 2: broke Codex's claim-naming fix (closed schema, probe verified) and the conflict-freeze (rotation DoS) -> rev 3
